message:
fix(e2e): correct PageCorrupt 1-indexed page convention and harden test assertions

Fix off-by-one bug in PageCorrupt corruption strategy:
- page_number is now treated as 1-indexed (matching SQLite convention)
  using checked_sub(1) for the zero-based offset calculation
- Add overflow-safe arithmetic via checked_mul/checked_add for seek offset
- Return explicit errors instead of silently skipping out-of-bounds pages

Harden test assertion patterns across the E2E suite:
- Replace panic!()-in-closures with AtomicBool tracking + error returns
  in WAL FEC tests (test_wal_fec_intact_fast_path, test_wal_fec_salt_
  mismatch_rejection) for better failure diagnostics
- Replace match...else{panic!} with assert!(matches!()) + if-let guards
  in recovery_single_page and corruption_scenarios tests
- Convert test_logging_json_format to return Result<()> with ? and map_err
  instead of unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(...))

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
